Settings
Account & sync
Not signed in. Reload the app to sign in.
✓ Connected
—
Sync queue
Items waiting to be written to Supabase. Use this to diagnose sync issues.
Pulls latest from server first, then pushes your local state. Use if data is missing from Supabase.
Manual backup
Export all farm data as a JSON backup. Import on another device or restore from backup.
Historical events
Import past grazing events to populate your fertility ledger and stats from day one.
Error log
Export a log of any errors the app has encountered. Share with the developer for analysis.
Danger zone
Farms
Forage types
Reference library for DM% and NPK removal values. Linked to feed types and harvest events.
Field mode
Choose which tiles appear on the field home screen. Settings are per-user.
Farm users
Invite a member
Setup import & export
Download a pre-formatted Excel template, fill it in (or paste data from your existing system), then import it to populate your setup automatically.
6 sheets: Animals · Animal_Classes · Animal_Groups · Locations · Feed_Types · Feed_Batches. Fill only what you need — empty sheets are skipped. Imports add new records and skip exact duplicates.
Operation
Weaning targets
Days from birth to target wean date, by species. Used to compute wean dates and flag overdue animals in the Weaning report.
Home screen stats
Choose which metrics appear on the Home screen. Changes take effect after saving.
NPK rates & prices
Excretion rates (lbs / 1,000 lbs BW / day)
Fertilizer prices ($/lb)
Recalculate NPK values on past events
When fertilizer prices change, update the dollar value of manure deposited on past events. NPK quantities (lbs) are not changed — only the $/acre value.
days
Manure production rates
Manure excreted per 1,000 lbs body weight per day (lbs, as-excreted wet weight)
Defaults: beef cattle ~65 lbs/1,000 lbs BW/day · 1 load = 8,000 lbs (4 tons)
App
Bump this when deploying (e.g. v1.2 → v1.3) to push updates to iOS.

Input & amendment products
Define the products you apply to pastures — fertilizers, lime, seed, herbicide, etc. NPK content is optional (enter 0 for non-fertilizer products).
Add product
NPK content (% by weight — leave 0 for non-fertilizer)
Animal units & rotation goals
Set target goals for each paddock metric. The rotation calendar uses these to color-code performance: green = met or exceeded, amber = within warning range, red = missed.
Default 1000 lbs. Used to calculate Animal Unit Days (AUDs) per rotation.
Grazing management
Default 26 lbs. Dry matter consumed by one AU per day.
Minimum height to graze to. Paddocks and forage types can override.
Percent of standing forage actually consumed. 50% is conservative; 60-70% for intensive management.
Per-metric goals
Amber threshold: if met% of target triggers amber (e.g. 75% means amber kicks in below 75% of your target).
For feed cost, higher = worse — set your maximum acceptable $/day and the threshold % above it that triggers amber.
Pasture recovery tracking
When moving off a paddock, record your estimated recovery window before the next graze. Helps track planned rest periods against actual rotation timing.
Default recovery window (days)
Earliest you'd graze this paddock again
Latest before the window closes
These defaults pre-fill the move wizard. You can adjust per-move during close.

Select your name to track your activity

Bale feed remaining right now (%)
Checkpoint for inferring grass DMI from this paddock. Lower % = more bales consumed = less inferred from grass.

Add sub-move
Enter times above for auto-calculation, or type hours directly. Common: milking ~2–2.5h · shade ~4h · overnight ~10h

Apply input / amendment
—
25%
Application summary
Cull animal
Animal will be removed from its group and marked inactive. All history (weight, calving, lineage) is preserved. You can reactivate from the animal record if needed.
Reset all data
This will permanently erase everything:
· All animals, groups, and classes
· All pasture events and locations
· All feed types, batches, and inventory
· All manure batches and input applications
· All tasks, users, and feedback
· All settings and operation name
If signed in to Supabase, cloud data will also be erased.
Consider exporting a backup first.

Reduces DMI of the most recent feeding to account for waste / leftover

Feed types
Define each type once — batches reference them for DM% and unit.

Hay analysis — optional
From a lab feed test. Used to credit uneaten bale OM as a fertility input. Leave blank if unknown.
Stored feed goal
Set how many days of stored feed you want to keep on hand. The progress bar on the feed screen measures against this goal.
Typical: 90d for winter, 180d for year-round hay-based operations
